task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
trace: To solve this problem, we first ensure that each number is counted only once by converting the input list to a set. Then, we sort the resulting set in descending order. If there are fewer than two distinct values, we return None; otherwise, we return the second-to-last element of the sorted set, which is the second largest value.
